none.justification: Matches the GT: it identifies that plain SET overwrites without WRONGTYPE, lists the two SET-only option-gated paths (GET via getGenericCommand and IFEQ/IFNE/IFDEQ/IFDNE direct), and names object.c:887 addReplyErrorObject(c, shared.wrongtypeerr) as the actual construction site.

ccodegraph.justification: Fully correct per GT: it distinguishes SET-specific call sites from the shared helper, includes both option families, notes ordinary SET has no checkType path, and pinpoints object.c:887 as the reply construction call.

codegraph.justification: It correctly finds the shared construction site and both relevant branches, but its call-chain summary incorrectly includes setnxCommand/setexCommand/psetexCommand as part of the WRONGTYPE-producing path, while the GT emphasizes those commands cannot set the GET or compare flags. That scope error is a real rubric gap even though the SET details are mostly right.

cbm.justification: Fully matches the GT: it states the shared construction site in checkType at object.c:887, identifies both SET-path triggers with the GET and compare-option branches, and explicitly rules out plain SET as producing WRONGTYPE.
